---
title: "Week 10 Milestone Params"
subtitle: "`r params$year` Report for `r params$herd`" 
author: "<img src='images/ac-logo.png' width='50' /> <br> Animal Clinic LLC"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    page-layout: full
    theme: flatly
    df_print: paged
    toc: true
    toc-location: left
    number-sections: false
    code-fold: true
execute:
  message: false
  warning: false
  echo: false
embed-resources: true

params: 
  herd: "MMW Dairy"
  year: "2025"

---

```{r}
#| label: setup
#| include: false

if (!require("pacman")) install.packages("pacman")
pacman::p_load(
  broom,
  tidyverse,
  dtplyr,
  gt,
  arrow,
  rmarkdown,
  lubridate,
  quarto, 
  arrow,
  survival,
  survminer,
  ggsurvfit,
  flextable,
  gtsummary,
  marginaleffects,
  scales,
  zoo
  )

# pull data 
events_formatted <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/events_formatted.parquet')

events_formatted <- events_formatted |>
  mutate(
    location_event = ifelse(
      source_file_path == "data/event_files/MV Dairy 1.16.26.csv",
      "MV Dairy",
      "MMW Dairy"
    )
  )

animals <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/animals.parquet') 

deno_cal <- read_parquet(
  '../data/intermediate_files/denominator_by_calendar_time_period.parquet') |> 
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic" & 
           calendar_time_period_type == "year") |> 
  select(-c(contains("dop"), day_of_phase_group))
  
# load functions
source('../functions/fxn_load_os_fxns.R')
fxn_load_os_fxns()

# Load Parnell CR fucntions from Github
cr_url <- "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ParnellPharma/ParnellFunctionsPublic/main/parnell_calculate_CR_master.R"
source(cr_url)

# create variables for inline code
farm_name <- unique(events_formatted$location_event)

#date_max_pull <- max(events_formatted$date_event, na.rm = TRUE)
date_max_pull <- events_formatted %>%
  filter(location_event == "MMW Dairy") %>%
  summarise(date_max_pull = max(date_event, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(date_max_pull)
date_min_pull <- date_max_pull - years(1)

date_max_pull

# set up key vectors 

```


```{r}
#| label: open-html-r -->
#| eval: false

# # 1. Define the file path
# file_paths <- c("images/farm_report_2024_herd_2108e.htm",
#                 "images/farm_report_2024_herd_2755f.htm",
#                 "images/farm_report_2025_herd_2108e.htm",
#                 "images/farm_report_2025_herd_2755f.htm"
#                 )
# 
# # 2. Open the browser
# walk(file_paths, browseURL)

```



# Report for `r params$year` and `r params$herd`

## Objective 

The objective of this milestone is to run a report over multiple herds
and years. Feel free to use existing code from previous milestones to
minimize the time spend creating new code. Try to include some in line R
code in the text that changes based on the herd you are working with.

The steps for this milestone are as follows (code sections for these
steps are below)

1.  Re-Run **step0_master_processing.R** if you did not do it last week
    to give you at least 2 herds to run a report on.

2.  I did not load any github functions automatically so if you want to
    call the functions we used previously you will need to load these.

3.  Set *params* in the yaml to include herd and year.

4.  Insert some tables and graphs from previous milestones into this
    document using tabs/columns or whatever layout you prefer. The data
    in these tables/graphs should be for each herd and for each year.

5.  Once you have a completed report for one herd and year you can use
    quarto to render reports for multiple herds/years by using the file
    ***milestone_10_render.R*** in the main directory (same one as step 0)  

    -   If you are using your own data adjust the herd names and years
        in the code of that document.
        -  You can also just run the report for 1 year by setting params to 1 year.

# Conception Risk Comparison

```{r}
deno_5y_tbl <- deno_cal %>%
  filter(calendar_time_period_type == "year") %>%
  filter(deno_type == "lact_basic") %>%
  mutate(
    Year = year(date_time_period_end),
    Lactation = ifelse(`Lactation Group` == "LACT > 0", "Adult Cows", "Heifer"),
    Location = ifelse(location_lact_list == "84634707", "MV Dairy", "MMW Dairy")
  ) %>%
  filter(Year >= max(Year, na.rm = TRUE) - 4) %>%
  select(
    Location,
    Year,
    Lactation,
    `Days at risk` = ct_animal_days_elig, 
    `DAR Animals`  = ct_animals,      
    Lactations     = ct_animal_lactations,
    Animals        = ct_animals
  ) %>%
  arrange(Location, desc(Year), Lactation)

events_formatted <- events_formatted |> 
  filter(date_event <= date_max_pull) 

cr_df <- events_formatted %>%
  mutate(year = year(date_event)) %>%
  fxn_create_CR_reports(
    grp_vars = c("location_event", "year", "lact_group_basic", "Rstandard")
  )

cr_table <- cr_df %>%
  mutate(
    Risk_Num = CR * 100,
    Plot_Data = CR * 100
  ) %>% 
  select(
    location_event, 
    Year = year, 
    Lactation = lact_group_basic, 
    `Not Bred Event`, 
    Preg = preg, 
    Total = total, 
    SPC, 
    Risk = Risk_Num, 
    CI_lower, 
    CI_upper,
    Plot_Data 
  ) %>%
  gt(groupname_col = "location_event") %>%
  
  fmt_number(columns = c(`Not Bred Event`, Preg, Total), decimals = 0, use_seps = TRUE) %>%
  fmt_number(columns = c(SPC, Risk), decimals = 1) %>%
  
  cols_merge_range(col_begin = CI_lower, col_end = CI_upper, sep = "–") %>%
  cols_nanoplot(
    columns = Plot_Data,
    plot_type = "bar",
    new_col_name = "Plot_Col",
    options = nanoplot_options(
      data_bar_fill = "orange",
      data_bar_stroke_color = "black",
      data_bar_stroke_width = 1
    )
  ) %>%
  cols_label(CI_lower = "95% CI", Plot_Col = "Plot") %>%
  
  summary_rows(
    groups = everything(), # Force summary on all groups
    columns = Risk,
    fns = list("Location Average" = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE)),
    fmt = list(~fmt_number(., decimals = 1)),
    missing_text = "—" 
  ) %>%
  
  tab_header(title = "Conception Risk Report") %>%
  tab_spanner(label = "Conception (%)", columns = c(Risk, CI_lower, Plot_Col)) %>%
  tab_options(
    summary_row.background.color = "lightblue",
    row_group.font.weight = "bold",
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    table.width = pct(100)
  ) %>%
  cols_align(align = "center", columns = everything())

# Pulling the numeric value for Adult Cows
adult_cr_val <- cr_df %>%
  filter(location_event == params$herd, 
         year == params$year, 
         lact_group_basic == "LACT > 0") %>%
  pull(CR) * 100

# Pulling the numeric value for Heifers
heifer_cr_val <- cr_df %>%
  filter(location_event == params$herd, 
         year == params$year, 
         lact_group_basic == "Heifer") %>%
  pull(CR) * 100

# Total animals from the deno table
total_animals <- deno_5y_tbl %>%
  filter(Location == params$herd, Year == params$year) %>%
  summarise(Total = sum(Animals, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  pull(Total)
```


This herd of `r format(total_animals, big.mark=",")` cows had a conception risk of `r round(adult_cr_val, 1)%` in lactating cows. In heifers of `r params$year`, the CR was `r round(heifer_cr_val, 1)`%. Compared to lactating cows, this heifer CR was higher than the highest conception risk calculated in adult cows.

# Tables


# Graphs


# Extension

For this week extension continue to create standard things you would
want to report or incorporate modeling techniques to explore the data

Ideas

-   Change the params to change the report by something other than herd or year.
-   Finalize your report that you would use on a routine basis in your practice.
